title: Everyone got a brand-new look
description: Every single person in the city — you included — now uses a brand-new, more detailed 3D character model, men and women alike, with much smoother walking, running and fighting. Your character (and armed cops and gang members) now raises weapons in a proper two-handed stance instead of waving them around, throws real punches, and finally stays down after a 'WASTED' instead of suddenly sitting up. If you've been hitting the gym, your bigger arms actually show on the new model. On top of all that the game runs noticeably smoother in crowded areas, and you can flip through every named character in the model viewer to see exactly who's who.

title: Blood, dismemberment, and police that won't quit
description: The violence just got a lot more visceral. Every shot now sprays blood, and a clean hit to the head or an arm tears it right off — bodies come apart. The police are relentless too: when a cruiser pulls up near you but can't quite reach, the officers now get out and come at you on foot — even at a single star — instead of just sitting in the car. They send the nearest cars after you, walk around buildings to reach you, and will now chase you all the way across the Vesper Bridge into the countryside. Regular drivers can cross the bridge now as well, and we fixed a nasty glitch where bumping into something under the bridge would fling you up on top of it.

title: A big round of fixes and polish
description: Lots of little things put right. You can now hot-wire the country cars cruising the dirt roads, not just the parked ones. Your saved garage car finally keeps everything you bolted onto it — spoiler, rims, neon, hood and engine — instead of forgetting it all on the next visit. Story characters show their names above their heads like everyone else, and the officer who steps out of the cruiser now matches the driver. People who want to buy your weed wave you over with a clear 'come here' and show up on the radar. We tamed the wild hair on some characters, widened the off-road rally so it flows better, and stopped the hidden country cash from paying out forever if you just walk in circles. The NPC list in the pause menu got a show/hide button for its filters so it's no longer so tall. The leaderboard now matches the money you actually have in your wallet. And blowing up your own car no longer crashes the game.

title: Take cover — and survive a fender-bender
description: A round of fixes to how the people of the city behave. Enemies — the police, the gangs and the army — can no longer shoot you straight through walls: duck behind a building and they'll hold their fire until they can actually see you again. And getting clipped by a passing car while on foot now hurts based on how fast it was going, instead of killing you outright from full health. We also tidied up some smaller things: run-over victims leave a blood pool like everyone else, gang members stand properly on the ground, and your wanted star keeps better track of when the police have truly lost you.

title: Meet the locals — and a living police force
description: Everyone in town is now a real person. Every pedestrian, shopkeeper, gang member, officer and soldier is a named individual with their own personality and home neighborhood, and their name floats above their head. Some folks will flag you down to buy weed, and many have something to say. The police are reborn as a living force: a fixed cast of named officers patrols the streets and now chases you along smart routes through the roads instead of grinding into walls. One of them is the Sheriff — in his own tan uniform — who runs the police radio: he calls in shots fired by your clothing and location, dispatches the nearest officers by name, escalates as your wanted level climbs, reports officers down and exploding cars, and summons the named Army squad at maximum heat. Officers now get out of the car to arrest you — at one star they order you to surrender, but resist (or push it to two stars) and they open fire. The siren grows louder the closer a chasing car gets, and your wanted star sticks while the police can still see you.

title: Cleaner, consistent objective markers
description: The glowing light beam that marks where to go — race checkpoints, deliveries, taxi fares, targets, story objectives and more — now shares one tidy, consistent look across the whole game. The marker left behind where a player fell carries the same style too, glowing red.

title: Cross the new bridge between the city and the countryside
description: A wide river strait now separates the city from the countryside, and a brand-new Golden Gate-style suspension bridge spans it. Drive over the bridge to cross between the two halves of the world, or take a boat through the strait beneath it. The off-road race has also moved out to the open fields just east of the bridge. We also closed an account loophole so a guest can no longer pose as a registered player.

title: Fixed the bandstand in the town square
description: The octagonal bandstand in Pine Hollow's town square had its white railings turned the wrong way — they jutted outward like spokes instead of running neatly between the columns. The railings now sit flush along each side the way they should, so the bandstand finally looks right.

title: Daily activities reset after a real break
description: The once-a-day activities — money mini-games like car exports and the scrap crusher, plus gym training — could leave you stuck. Their 'come back tomorrow' timer ran on the in-game clock, which freezes while the game is closed, so stepping away in real life and coming back later didn't help. Now that timer also clears after you've been away for a little while in real life, so returning after a break lets you jump straight back into your favorite activities.

title: Smoother countryside
description: More performance work out in the country: the ranch house, the farm tractors and other big rural objects are now drawn far more efficiently, so the frame rate stays high around the farms and the village of Pine Hollow — even on weaker devices. Nothing looks any different; it just runs lighter.

title: Every part of the map now has a name
description: The whole world is mapped out by name now. The city is split into named neighborhoods — Downtown, Eastside, Westside, Harborview, Sunset Bluffs and more — and the countryside has its own named areas, from Meadowbrook and Drycreek Farms to Mount Vesper, Whispering Pines and the village of Pine Hollow. Open the full map (M) to see every name written across it in sharp purple lettering, and a little banner by the radar announces each area as you cross into it. The eastern countryside also runs a touch smoother.

title: Cleaner pause menu, a map shortcut and a Mini-Games guide
description: The pause menu is tidier: RESUME, INFO, SETTINGS and QUIT. INFO now gathers the Leaderboard, your Transactions and the Updates, plus two new things — a MAP shortcut that opens the full map, and a MINI-GAMES guide that lists every activity in the game with exactly how much it pays, what it costs and its timers, so you always know what's worth your time. Fullscreen moved into Settings. We also tuned some payouts: the scrap crusher and car exports pay more, dance nights tip better, and the boat race pays a little extra for braving the mines. And a weed-farm fix: a delivery run will always have buyers around to sell to — you can no longer get stuck holding a stash with nobody to deal it to.

title: New intro screen and a grittier, more cinematic look
description: The game now opens with a stylish black title card — 'André N. Darcie presents' — that lights up while the world and the leaderboard load in the background, so the menu is ready and Play drops you straight in. The whole game also got a more grounded, filmic colour grade (less candy-bright, a touch moodier), a subtle moving film grain, and smoother, cleaner edges. Buildings show windows on every side now, and there's general polish across the streets. Don't like the grain? Turn it off in Settings → Graphics → Film grain.

title: City parks got a serious glow-up
description: The little city squares are no longer a patch of green with a fountain. They now have proper textured grass, stone walkways and a paved plaza around the fountain, a trimmed hedge border with openings at the paths, raised flower beds bursting with colour, planters framing the entrances, and more trees, bushes, lamps and benches — a real place to hang out.

title: Dress up at the new clothing store
description: A new clothing store, THREADS, has opened in the city — look for the pink storefront on the map. Step inside, stand on the podium in front of the mirror and press the action button to open the wardrobe: pick the colour of your shirt, pants and shoes, and top it off with a hat (cap or beanie) and shades. Everything is previewed live on you as you turn on the podium, and you pay only for what you keep. Your outfit is saved to your account, so you'll keep your look on any device.

title: Aim assist only helps with nearby enemies
description: The aim assist is now more honest: it only nudges your aim toward enemies who are close by and roughly on your level. No more snapping toward someone far away — or toward people down on the street while you're up on a rooftop. As before, you can turn it off entirely in Settings → Gameplay.

title: New over-the-shoulder aim mode
description: Gunfights on foot got a big upgrade. Press the right mouse button — or tap the new AIM button on phones — to enter aim mode: the camera pulls in close over your shoulder, a crosshair appears, and your shots now land exactly where you point, including up and down. While aiming, your character grips the gun in a realistic two-handed stance; the rest of the time the gun stays holstered and you walk normally, drawing it only when you aim or fire. A gentle aim assist helps you line up targets — and if you'd rather aim entirely by hand, you can switch it off in Settings → Gameplay.

title: Country folk react like everyone else now
description: The folks out in the countryside were strangely invincible — you could shoot or punch them and nothing happened. Not any more: they now take hits, go down with a tumble and a blood pool just like city pedestrians, and a fresh face wanders back to the farm afterwards. The derelict houses on the village outskirts also got a proper makeover — caved-in roofs, boarded-up windows and doors hanging off their hinges, so they finally look abandoned. And the spare tractor that was parked on top of the welcome sign has been moved aside.

title: The police now chase you across the water
description: Think you can shake the cops by diving into the sea or speeding off in a boat? Not any more. While you're wanted and out on the water, a police boat now roars out from the open sea and hunts you down — it rams your boat, and the officer aboard opens fire once you've picked up a couple of stars. The boats can't beach themselves, so reaching the shore is still your way out. They show up on your radar so you can see them closing in.

title: Weapon pickups give you one gun, not the whole armory
description: Grabbing the weapon pickup in the park (and the loot stash on a rooftop) used to instantly hand you every weapon in the game for free. Now it gives you a pistol to get started — the rest of the arsenal comes from the gun shop or from the weapon stashes hidden around the edges of the map. The temporary full arsenal you get during the rocket frenzy is unchanged.

title: Beach & island weed runs, island treasure, and a sting that bites
description: The weed delivery run reaches new ground. There are now buyers waiting down on the city beach and out on the far island — you'll need a boat or a plane to make that island drop. Hidden across the island is a secret cache of heavy weapons and cash, including a fat wad of bills buried right on top of the island's hill. And mind your heat: when a deal turns out to be a police sting, an undercover cop now nabs you on the spot and hauls you off for a crooked-cop shakedown, wherever the deal went down.

title: Backpack on the bike, fairer daily jobs and a flat hospital bill
description: Three fixes. Your weed delivery backpack now rides on your back when you hop on the motorcycle, instead of vanishing the moment you got on. The 'once a day' money jobs (like the off-road race) now unlock properly when a new in-game day comes around — before, they could stay stubbornly locked even after time had clearly moved on. And the hospital bill when you die is now a flat $100 instead of a big chunk of your cash, so going down while rich no longer costs you a fortune.

title: Planes stop blowing up over the village
description: Flying over the countryside village, your plane could suddenly explode in mid-air for no reason — it was clipping the invisible top of the church tower. Planes now cruise cleanly over every rooftop, tower and windmill; you'll only crash if you're flying low enough to actually hit something.

title: A living countryside and two made-over hideouts
description: The rural side of the map got a big pass. Locals out in the country now scatter and run when you start shooting near them. Scattered around the village are hidden stashes of cash and weapons to dig up on foot, a cluster of abandoned houses swallowed by the woods, and a second tractor to commandeer. The big hill is a real climb now — you need a run-up to get a car over it and you'll pick up speed barrelling down the far side. Indoors, the hospital and your buyable country house were rebuilt with proper furniture, lighting and detail, and you can't walk through the furniture any more. Plus: take a tumble off a rooftop and your character now flops down like a ragdoll, and dropped cash looks like a real wad of bills.

title: Fixed the black ground on phones
description: On mobile, if you switched away from the game and came back, the ground sometimes turned completely black. It now redraws itself the moment you return, so the streets and countryside look right again.

title: Sharper streets and a smarter camera
description: A round of polish across the city. Building windows look far more realistic now — proper tinted glass instead of cartoon blue, and they line up cleanly instead of getting cut off at the top and bottom. The little city parks got a real makeover too, each with a stone fountain, benches, street lamps and trees. On foot, the camera now eases back behind you as you walk, just like it does when you're driving. Your wanted stars only show up once you've actually got heat on you. And the whole game runs a bit smoother.

title: Two Brazilian classics hit the streets
description: Local traffic just got some Brazilian flavour. A white VW Kombi van and a boxy little Fiat Uno now cruise the streets among the regular cars — and like any ride, you can pull the driver out and take one for yourself.

title: A village square to call home
description: The countryside village has a proper heart now: a main square with a little church and a bandstand ringed by benches. A calm spot to wander through between jobs.

title: Blood money: cash in on the fallen
description: Death now leaves a mark. When anyone dies their hospital bill spills onto the ground as a glowing pool of blood, tagged with their name — and you'll see the pools left behind by other players from all over the world. Be the first to walk over one and the cash is yours. So when you go down, somebody out there is coming to collect what you dropped. Better get there first.

title: A ride home and some grow-op banter
description: There's now a motorcycle parked outside the weed farm's gate — hop on for the delivery run, and your backpack tucks away while you ride. Buyers no longer disappear the moment you sell to them; they stick around, and every deal plays a quick little hand-off where the buyer thanks you with one of dozens of random, mouthy one-liners. We also smoothed out a few rough edges: vehicles can't barge through the farm gate onto your crop anymore, and a water bucket no longer tags along halfway across the map after you leave.

title: Run your own weed operation
description: The hidden farm is now a full business. Buy seeds in three strains at the General Store — each grows, drinks and pays differently — feed and water your plants, then dry and cure the harvest for a fatter price. You no longer sell on the spot: stash the crop, strap on a backpack and deliver it to buyers out in the country and the city. Watch the heat, though — push your luck and a deal can turn into a police sting or a raid on the farm.

title: Farm upgrades & a proper store
description: Reinvest your earnings at the grow shack: a bigger can, a garden hose, or sprinklers that water the beds for you. The farm is now lit at night so you can work after dark, and the General Store got a full glow-up inside and out.

title: The General Store is open
description: Drop by the country store on the village main street and step inside. Buy seed packs at the counter — and you'll want to, because the garden now needs seeds before you can plant anything.

title: Thicker forest, smoother menus
description: The countryside is wilder now, with denser pine forest spreading across the peninsula. The pause menu also lays out better on phones, so it's easier to read and tap on the go.

title: See what's new
description: This very screen is new! The pause menu now has an Updates panel that lists everything we add to the game, in plain language. A little NEW badge lets you know whenever there's something fresh to read.

title: All-new pause menu
description: Hit pause at any time to open a proper menu. From one place you can check the global leaderboard, browse your full money history, change graphics and sound, go fullscreen, switch account, or quit to the title screen.

title: Sail to Paradise Isle
description: Grab a boat and head out to sea. There's a brand-new tropical island waiting offshore that you can land on and explore on foot.

title: Louder, sharper sound
description: Police sirens now wail like the real thing, and the whole world is full of new sound effects that react to what's happening around you.

title: A livelier countryside
description: The rural village got a football pitch behind the church, friendlier locals wandering the streets, and the occasional truck rumbling down the dirt roads. The mountain is smoother to drive over, too.

title: First-person view
description: Press C to switch to a first-person camera. On foot you'll see your own hands and weapon; behind the wheel you get a detailed cockpit with hands gripping the steering wheel.

title: New town: Pine Hollow
description: A whole rural town has sprung up east of the mountain. Explore Pine Hollow with its dense pine forest, fort and farms — you can even climb aboard a tractor and drive it.

title: A hidden grow-op
description: There's a secret weed-farm mini-game tucked away in the countryside, waiting for players who go exploring off the beaten path.

title: More to do, more to win
description: New activities, weapons and a fresh island arrived — each activity now has its own leaderboard. Boat races also got explosive water mines to dodge.

title: Your progress is saved
description: Your money and progress now stick around between sessions, so you can pick up right where you left off. The in-game clock is synced to real time and shows the day of the week.

title: Full map & smoother play
description: Press M to open a full map of the world, with clearer icons on the minimap. The whole game also runs noticeably smoother now.

title: Guns & speedboats
description: Pick up guns and tear across the water in a Vice City-style speedboat that leaves a foamy wake behind it.

title: Welcome to Tiny Theft Auto
description: The world opened up: a house and safe house to call home, motorcycles to ride, street races to win, a gym to train at, plus a prison, a hospital, talking pedestrians and the very first story missions.
